status: accepted
created_on: 2025-06-13

# ADR-000: The Score

## Decision

This project uses Orchestra â€” a software development lifecycle encoded for agents. PRDs are the unit of work. Every significant piece of work has a PRD before a spec, and a spec before implementation.

## Rationale

Without a written PRD, work drifts. Without a spec, agents have no contract to
execute against. Without Gherkin, there is no definition of done an agent can verify.

The `.orchestra/` folder is the shared knowledge base. Agents read it. Humans update
it at each gate. The score doesn't change mid-performance without a new ADR.

## Consequences

- No implementation without an approved spec
- No spec without an approved PRD
- All significant architectural decisions are recorded as ADRs
/.orchestra/adr/ADR-001-python-cli-shell:
---
status: accepted
created_on: 2025-06-13
---

# ADR-001: Python CLI that shells out to the git binary

## Context

`relnotes` is a command-line tool that reads a git repository's commit history
between two tags and produces markdown release notes. Before writing the M1
spec we needed to settle three foundational choices that determine every
downstream file path and the distribution story: the implementation language,
how the tool accesses git, and the supported range syntax.

Two stacks were weighed. **Go** produces a single static binary (trivial install
in M4) but is slower to write. **Python** is faster to author and ubiquitous on
developer machines, at the cost of a heavier install/runtime story. For git
access, the options were shelling out to the installed `git` binary (portable,
zero native dependencies, matches the mental model of maintainers) versus a
library binding (e.g. pygit2/GitPython â€” native deps, more surface area).

## Decision

Build `relnotes` in **Python**, accessing git by **shelling out to the `git`
binary** via `subprocess`. M1 supports two range forms: `A..B` and bare `A`
(treated as `A..HEAD`).

Python was chosen for authoring speed and ubiquity; the heavier install story is
accepted and will be addressed in M4 (e.g. pipx / single-file build). Shelling
out to `git` avoids native dependencies, keeps the tool portable, and means the
tool resolves revisions exactly as the user's own `git` does. The narrow range
syntax keeps M1 focused; richer revision selection (`...`, multiple ranges,
arbitrary revisions) is deferred.

## Consequences

- The project is a Python package. Tooling is `uv` + `pytest`; tests follow the
  three-tier policy (unit with mocked subprocess, integration against a real
  fixture git repo, E2E invoking the CLI).
- `git` must be on the user's `PATH` at runtime. Its absence is a first-class
  error case the tool must handle clearly. This is a documented runtime
  dependency, not a bug.
- Git access is centralized behind one module (a `git` wrapper) so the
  subprocess boundary is mockable in unit tests and swappable later if a binding
  is ever preferred. No other module shells out directly.
- Range parsing is centralized: only `A..B` and bare `A` are accepted in M1;
  anything else is a clear usage error. M2+ may widen this.
- Distribution (M4) must solve Python packaging/install; that work is explicitly
  deferred and should not block M1â€“M3.

# 2025-06-13: M1 planning complete â€” stack decided, spec/gherkin/plan drafted

## Summary

Human resolved gate 96c3a3f9: build in **Python**, keep **shell-out to the git
binary**, and the **`A..B` / bare-`A`** range syntax for M1. Recorded the
decision as ADR-001, approved the M1 PRD, and drove the full planning loop â€”
spec, Gherkin scenarios, and a numbered implementation plan are all drafted and
ready for the pre-implementation gate.

## What shipped

- **ADR-001** â€” Python CLI that shells out to git; documents the stack, the
  git-access boundary, the runtime `git`-on-PATH dependency, and the narrow M1
  range syntax.
- **M1 PRD** â†’ `status: approved`, materials table filled with concrete
  `src/relnotes/*.py` and `tests/**` paths.
- **spec.md** â€” module pipeline (`cli â†’ ranges â†’ git â†’ models â†’ render â†’ errors`),
  a parse-friendly `git log` format using `%x1f`/`%x1e` control bytes, full
  three-tier TDD strategy, deliverables, acceptance criteria, and risks.
- **gherkin-spec.md** â€” 8 scenarios covering explicit range, bare-tagâ†’HEAD,
  unknown tag, empty range, outside-repo, git-missing, malformed range, and
  `--help`/`--version`.
- **plan.md** â€” 10 dependency-ordered steps, test-before-implementation at every
  stage.
- Roadmap M1 â†’ In Progress.

## Key decisions & rationale

**Single subprocess boundary.** Only `git.py` shells out; everything else is pure
and unit-testable. This keeps the mock surface tiny and makes the integration
tier (real git, real fixture repo) the single place where git reality is
verified.

**Control-byte log format.** Using `%x1f`/`%x1e` as field/record separators means
commit subjects can contain anything (including `|`, newlines) without breaking
the parser â€” a classic release-notes footgun avoided up front.

**M1 stays flat on purpose.** No grouping/noise/breaking-change logic â€” that is
M2. M1 exists to de-risk plumbing and lock the output contract.

## What comes next

Pre-implementation gate: approve spec + plan, then `/orchestra-implement
m1-skeleton`. Nothing gets coded until that gate clears.

# 2025-06-13: Project setup, roadmap, and M1 PRD

## Summary

Bootstrapped the `relnotes` project from an empty directory: scaffolded the
`.orchestra/` knowledge base, wrote the README Brief, drafted a four-milestone
roadmap, and authored a full PRD for the first milestone (M1 â€” walking
skeleton). Stopped at the first genuine human gate: three architecture/product
decisions that drive every downstream file path.

## What shipped

- `.orchestra/` scaffold: README, ADR-000, PRD/spec templates, devlog + uml dirs
- Root `README.md` with a filled-in Brief (vision + audience) and `CLAUDE.md`
- `.orchestra/roadmap.md` â€” four milestones (M1 skeleton â†’ M2 curation â†’ M3 config â†’ M4 distribution)
- Stub PRDs for M2â€“M4
- Full PRD for **M1 â€” Walking Skeleton** with scope, success criteria, and an explicit Open Decisions section

## Key decisions & rationale

**Milestone shape.** The headline value (grouping, noise filtering, breaking
changes) is concentrated in M2, not M1. I deliberately scoped M1 as a true
walking skeleton â€” tag range â†’ commit list â†’ flat markdown â€” so we de-risk the
plumbing (range resolution across tag/branch/HEAD, robust git access, error
handling, output contract) before layering curation. A demoable, pipeable
skeleton beats a half-built curator.

**Deferred the genuine choices to a human.** Rather than silently picking a
language, I surfaced three real decisions in the M1 PRD: implementation stack,
git access method, and the range-syntax surface. These are irreversible-ish and
the human owns them. My recommendations (Go + shell out to `git` + `A..B`/bare-`A`)
are noted but not assumed.

## What comes next

1. Human resolves the three open decisions at the gate.
2. Record the choice as an ADR (stack + git access).
3. Fill the M1 materials table with concrete file paths.
4. Proceed to spec â†’ gherkin â†’ plan for M1.

# Gherkin Scenarios: M1 â€” Walking Skeleton: Tag Range â†’ Commits â†’ Markdown

> Source: ./spec.md
> Generated: 2025-06-13

```gherkin
Feature: Walking skeleton release notes
  Given a git tag range, relnotes reads the commits between the tags and prints
  a flat markdown document listing them to stdout.

  Background:
    Given a git repository with tags "v1.0.0" and "v1.1.0"
    And three commits exist that are reachable from "v1.1.0" but not "v1.0.0"

  Scenario: Generate notes for an explicit tag range
    When I run "relnotes v1.0.0..v1.1.0"
    Then the command exits with code 0
    And stdout is valid markdown with a title containing the range and a date
    And stdout contains one bullet per commit with its subject and short SHA
    And exactly the three commits in the range are listed

  Scenario: Default the upper bound to HEAD
    Given two more commits exist on HEAD after "v1.1.0"
    When I run "relnotes v1.1.0"
    Then the command exits with code 0
    And the range is treated as "v1.1.0..HEAD"
    And only the two commits after "v1.1.0" are listed

  Scenario: Unknown tag is reported clearly
    When I run "relnotes v9.9.9..v1.1.0"
    Then the command exits with a non-zero code
    And stderr contains a clear message naming the unknown ref "v9.9.9"
    And no Python traceback is printed

  Scenario: Empty range is reported clearly
    When I run "relnotes v1.1.0..v1.1.0"
    Then the command exits with a non-zero code
    And stderr explains that no commits were found in the range

  Scenario: Running outside a git repository
    Given the current directory is not inside a git repository
    When I run "relnotes v1.0.0..v1.1.0"
    Then the command exits with a non-zero code
    And stderr explains that this is not a git repository

  Scenario: The git binary is not installed
    Given the "git" binary is not available on PATH
    When I run "relnotes v1.0.0..v1.1.0"
    Then the command exits with a non-zero code
    And stderr explains that git could not be found

  Scenario: Malformed range syntax is rejected
    When I run "relnotes v1.0.0...v1.1.0"
    Then the command exits with code 2
    And stderr explains the supported range forms "A..B" and "A"

  Scenario: Discoverability flags work
    When I run "relnotes --help"
    Then the command exits with code 0
    And stdout describes the usage and the range argument
    When I run "relnotes --version"
    Then the command exits with code 0
    And stdout prints the relnotes version
```

# Implementation Plan: M1 â€” Walking Skeleton

> Spec: [spec.md](./spec.md) Â· Gherkin: [gherkin-spec.md](./gherkin-spec.md)

TDD throughout: each test file is committed before the implementation it covers.
Run `uv run pytest` (unit) green before every commit.

1. **Scaffold the package.** Create `pyproject.toml` (package `relnotes`, `src/`
   layout, console script `relnotes = "relnotes.cli:main"`, deps: dev-only
   `pytest`; register `integration` and `e2e` pytest markers, default run
   excludes them). Create `src/relnotes/__init__.py` with `__version__`.
   `uv sync`. Commit.

2. **Define errors and model (no behavior, no tests needed yet).** Create
   `src/relnotes/errors.py` with `RelnotesError` base and subclasses:
   `UsageError` (exit 2), `NotARepoError`, `GitNotFoundError`, `UnknownRefError`,
   `EmptyRangeError` (exit 1). Create `src/relnotes/models.py` with a frozen
   `Commit` dataclass (`sha`, `short_sha`, `subject`, `author`, `iso_date`).
   Commit.

3. **Range parsing (TDD).** Write `tests/unit/test_range.py`: `A..B`â†’(`A`,`B`);
   `A`â†’(`A`,`HEAD`); reject `..B`, `A..`, `A...B`, `""`, whitespace. Commit
   tests. Implement `src/relnotes/ranges.py::parse_range(arg)->tuple[str,str]`
   raising `UsageError` on rejects. Commit.

4. **Git wrapper (TDD, subprocess mocked).** Write `tests/unit/test_git.py`
   monkeypatching `subprocess.run`: parse canned `%x1f/%x1e` log output into
   `Commit`s; simulate unknown ref â†’ `UnknownRefError`; not-a-repo â†’
   `NotARepoError`; `FileNotFoundError` â†’ `GitNotFoundError`. Commit tests.
   Implement `src/relnotes/git.py`: `assert_repo()`, `verify_ref(ref)`,
   `read_commits(low, high) -> list[Commit]` (the only module that calls
   `subprocess`). Commit.

5. **Markdown renderer (TDD, clock monkeypatched).** Write
   `tests/unit/test_render.py`: title line with range + fixed date; one
   `- {subject} ({short_sha})` bullet per commit; stable ordering. Commit tests.
   Implement `src/relnotes/render.py::render(range_str, commits)->str`. Commit.

6. **CLI wiring (TDD).** Write `tests/unit/test_cli.py` using a `main(argv)` with
   captured stdout/stderr: success path (git layer mocked) prints markdown, exit
   0; each error class â†’ documented exit code + stderr message, no traceback;
   `--help`/`--version`. Commit tests. Implement `src/relnotes/cli.py::main`:
   parse args, `parse_range`, `assert_repo`, `verify_ref` both ends,
   `read_commits`, empty result â†’ `EmptyRangeError`, `render`, print to stdout;
   wrap exceptions â†’ exit codes. Commit.

7. **Integration tests (real git).** Add `tests/conftest.py` with a `fixture_repo`
   helper that `git init`s a repo in `tmp_path`, makes commits, and tags them.
   Write `tests/integration/test_git_real.py` (`@pytest.mark.integration`): real
   `read_commits` for `A..B` and bare `A`; unknown tag; empty range. No mocks at
   the git boundary. Run `uv run pytest -m integration` green. Commit.

8. **E2E tests (real CLI).** Write `tests/e2e/test_cli_e2e.py`
   (`@pytest.mark.e2e`): `subprocess.run(["relnotes", ...])` in a fixture repo â€”
   success stdout+exit 0; unknown tag non-zero+stderr; outside-repo error;
   `--version`/`--help`. Run `uv run pytest -m e2e` green. Commit.

9. **Developer README.** Fill README Usage: dev install (`uv sync`), run example
   with sample output, runtime `git`-on-PATH note. Commit.

10. **Close-out.** Run `uv run pytest` and `uv run pytest -m "integration or
    e2e"` all green. Update PRD/spec materials tables to Done, roadmap M1 â†’
    Done, write a devlog entry. Hand off to review.

# M1 â€” Walking Skeleton: Tag Range â†’ Commits â†’ Markdown

## Objective

Ship the smallest end-to-end `relnotes` that proves the core plumbing: given a
git tag range, read the commits between the two tags and emit a valid markdown
document listing them. No curation yet â€” this milestone exists to nail the CLI
contract, the git access, the range resolution, and the markdown output shape
that M2's curation will build on.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `relnotes v1.2.0..v1.3.0` run inside a git repo prints markdown listing every commit reachable from `v1.3.0` but not `v1.2.0`
- [ ] A single-argument form (`relnotes v1.2.0`) defaults the upper bound to `HEAD`
- [ ] If a tag does not exist or the range is empty, the tool exits non-zero with a clear, actionable error message (not a stack trace)
- [ ] Output is well-formed markdown with a title (range + date) and one bullet per commit (subject + short SHA)
- [ ] Output goes to stdout so it can be piped or redirected
- [ ] Running outside a git repository produces a clear error
- [ ] `relnotes --help` and `relnotes --version` work

## Context

Part of the [relnotes Roadmap](../../roadmap.md). This is milestone 1 of 4. The
product's headline value â€” grouping, noise filtering, breaking-change
highlighting â€” lands in M2. M1 deliberately stops at a flat list so we validate
the riskiest plumbing (range resolution across tag/branch/HEAD inputs, robust
git access, error handling) before layering curation logic on top. A working
skeleton we can demo and pipe is worth more than a half-built curator.

## Scope

**In scope**
- CLI entry point with argument parsing, `--help`, `--version`
- Resolve a tag range (`A..B`, `A` â†’ `A..HEAD`) to a commit list
- Read commit metadata: SHA, subject, author, date
- Render a flat markdown document to stdout
- Error handling for: missing tags, empty range, not-a-git-repo, invalid range syntax

**Out of scope (deferred)**
- Any grouping, categorization, or classification (M2)
- Noise filtering (M2)
- Breaking-change detection (M2)
- Config files, templates, custom output destinations (M3)
- Packaging and distribution (M4)

## Materials

| Deliverable | Location | Status |
|-------------|----------|--------|
| CLI entry point & arg parsing | `src/relnotes/cli.py` | Authored |
| Git wrapper (range resolution + commit reader) | `src/relnotes/git.py` | Authored |
| Commit model | `src/relnotes/models.py` | Authored |
| Markdown renderer (flat list) | `src/relnotes/render.py` | Authored |
| Errors & exit codes | `src/relnotes/errors.py` | Authored |
| Package metadata / entry point | `pyproject.toml` | Authored |
| Unit tests | `tests/unit/` | Authored |
| Integration tests (fixture repo) | `tests/integration/` | Authored |
| E2E tests (CLI invocation) | `tests/e2e/` | Authored |
| Developer README (build & run) | `README.md` | Authored |

## Resolved Decisions (gate 96c3a3f9, recorded in ADR-001)

1. **Implementation stack:** **Python** (package built with `uv`, tested with
   `pytest`).
2. **Git access method:** **Shell out to the `git` binary** via `subprocess`,
   centralized behind `src/relnotes/git.py`.
3. **Range syntax surface (M1):** Support `A..B` and bare `A` (â†’ `A..HEAD`).
   Defer `...`, multiple ranges, and arbitrary revisions.

See [ADR-001](../../adr/ADR-001-python-cli-shell-out-to-git.md).

## References

- [relnotes Roadmap](../../roadmap.md)
- ADR-000: [The Score](../../adr/ADR-000-the-score.md)

## Notes

The three open decisions were resolved at gate 96c3a3f9 (Python + shell-out to
git + `A..B`/bare-`A`) and recorded in ADR-001. The materials table now carries
concrete file paths. PRD approved â€” proceeding to spec.

# M1 â€” Walking Skeleton: Tag Range â†’ Commits â†’ Markdown

> PRD: [prd.md](./prd.md)
> ADR: [ADR-001 â€” Python CLI that shells out to git](../../adr/ADR-001-python-cli-shell-out-to-git.md)

## Objective

Ship the smallest end-to-end `relnotes`: given a git tag range, read the commits
between the two tags and emit a valid markdown document listing them. No
curation â€” this validates the CLI contract, git access, range resolution,
markdown output shape, and error handling that M2 will build on.

## Architecture

A thin pipeline, each stage in its own module so the subprocess boundary is
isolated and mockable:

```
cli.py        argv â†’ parsed args (range string, options)
  â””â”€ range parsing: "A..B" â†’ (A, B); "A" â†’ (A, "HEAD")
git.py        (low, high) â†’ list[Commit]   [the ONLY module that runs subprocess]
  â””â”€ verifies repo, verifies refs exist, runs `git log low..high`
models.py     Commit dataclass (sha, short_sha, subject, author, iso_date)
render.py     (range, list[Commit]) â†’ markdown string
errors.py     RelnotesError hierarchy â†’ mapped to exit codes in cli.py
```

Data flows one direction; no stage reaches backward. `cli.py` is the only place
that knows about `argv`, exit codes, and stdout/stderr.

### Git command contract

The git wrapper uses a stable, parse-friendly format. One commit per record,
fields separated by an unambiguous delimiter:

```
git log <low>..<high> --no-merges=NO  --pretty=format:%H%x1f%h%x1f%s%x1f%an%x1f%aI%x1e
```

- M1 does **not** filter merges (that is M2 noise handling) â€” every commit in the
  range is listed.
- `%x1f` (unit separator) splits fields; `%x1e` (record separator) splits commits.
  These bytes never appear in commit text, so no escaping is needed.
- Ref existence is checked first with `git rev-parse --verify --quiet <ref>^{commit}`
  so we produce a clean "unknown tag" error instead of a raw git failure.
- Repo presence is checked with `git rev-parse --git-dir`.
- `git` absence (FileNotFoundError from subprocess) maps to a clear error.

### Range resolution semantics

- `A..B` â†’ commits reachable from `B` but not `A` (git's `A..B`).
- `A` â†’ treated as `A..HEAD`.
- Empty/whitespace argument, or a string with `...`, multiple `..`, or a leading/
  trailing empty side (`..B`, `A..`) â†’ usage error (exit 2). M1 keeps this narrow.

## Approach

### Step 1: Project skeleton & tooling
Create `pyproject.toml` (package `relnotes`, `src/` layout, console-script entry
point `relnotes = "relnotes.cli:main"`), configure `pytest` with markers
`integration` and `e2e` registered (default run excludes them). Add `errors.py`
with the exception hierarchy and `models.py` with the `Commit` dataclass. These
have no behavior to mock and unblock everything else.

### Step 2: Range parsing (TDD)
Write `tests/unit/test_range.py` first covering `A..B`, bare `A`â†’`A..HEAD`, and
each rejected form. Then implement `parse_range(arg) -> (low, high)` in `cli.py`
(or a small `ranges.py` helper imported by `cli.py`).

### Step 3: Git wrapper (TDD)
Write `tests/unit/test_git.py` first, mocking `subprocess.run` (via
`monkeypatch`) to return canned `git log` output and to simulate: success,
unknown ref, not-a-repo, and `git` not installed. Then implement `git.py`:
`assert_repo()`, `verify_ref(ref)`, `read_commits(low, high) -> list[Commit]`,
parsing the `%x1f/%x1e` format into `Commit` objects.

### Step 4: Markdown renderer (TDD)
Write `tests/unit/test_render.py` first: title line with range + generation date,
one bullet per commit (`- {subject} ({short_sha})`), and the empty-range case
(handled as an error upstream, but renderer must still produce valid output for a
non-empty list deterministically). Then implement `render.py`.

### Step 5: CLI wiring & errorâ†’exit-code mapping (TDD at unit level)
Write `tests/unit/test_cli.py` first using `click.testing`/`CliRunner` or a
`main(argv)` function with captured streams, asserting exit codes and stderr
messages for each error class and a success path (mocked git). Then implement
`main()`: parse args, call the pipeline, print markdown to stdout, map exceptions
to exit codes, support `--help` and `--version`.

### Step 6: Integration tests against a real fixture repo
Build a throwaway git repo in a `tmp_path` fixture (real `git init`, commits,
`git tag`), then call `read_commits` and the full pipeline with **no mocks** at
the git boundary. Covers real `git log` output shape, real tag resolution, and
the real "unknown tag"/"empty range" behavior.

### Step 7: E2E tests invoking the installed CLI
Invoke `relnotes` as a subprocess (`subprocess.run(["relnotes", "v1..v2"], ...)`)
inside a real fixture repo and assert on stdout markdown, exit codes, and stderr.
Covers entry-point wiring, arg parsing, and stream/exit-code behavior end to end.

### Step 8: Developer README
Fill the README Usage section: install for dev (`uv sync`), run, example output,
and the runtime `git` dependency note.

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests (`tests/unit/`, default `uv run pytest`)
- `test_range.py` â€” range parsing: `A..B`, bare `A`, all rejected forms.
- `test_git.py` â€” git wrapper with `subprocess.run` mocked: success parse, unknown
  ref, not-a-repo, `git` missing. **Mocks the subprocess boundary** â€” fast,
  deterministic.
- `test_render.py` â€” markdown shape: title, bullets, ordering, date formatting
  (clock monkeypatched for determinism).
- `test_cli.py` â€” exit-code & stderr mapping per error class, success path (git
  layer mocked).

### Integration Tests (`tests/integration/`, `uv run pytest -m integration`)
- `test_git_real.py` â€” **real `git` binary against a real fixture repo** built in
  `tmp_path`. Verifies `git log` output shape, tag/HEAD resolution, `A..B` vs
  bare-`A`, unknown-tag error, empty-range detection. This is the tier that
  catches reality the mocks encode assumptions about (e.g. git output format,
  exit codes, ref-not-found stderr).

### E2E Tests (`tests/e2e/`, `uv run pytest -m e2e`)
- `test_cli_e2e.py` â€” invoke the **installed `relnotes` console script** as a
  subprocess in a real fixture repo. Assert: success prints markdown to stdout
  and exits 0; unknown tag exits non-zero with message on stderr; running outside
  a repo errors clearly; `--version`/`--help` work.

TDD commit ordering (mandatory): each test file is committed before the
implementation file it covers (Steps 2â€“7).

## Deliverables

| File | Purpose | Status |
|------|---------|--------|
| `pyproject.toml` | Package metadata, entry point, pytest markers | Authored |
| `src/relnotes/__init__.py` | Package + `__version__` | Authored |
| `src/relnotes/__main__.py` | `python -m relnotes` entry | Authored |
| `src/relnotes/errors.py` | `RelnotesError` hierarchy | Authored |
| `src/relnotes/models.py` | `Commit` dataclass | Authored |
| `src/relnotes/ranges.py` | `parse_range` helper | Authored |
| `src/relnotes/git.py` | Subprocess git wrapper | Authored |
| `src/relnotes/render.py` | Flat markdown renderer | Authored |
| `src/relnotes/cli.py` | Entry point, arg parsing, exit-code mapping | Authored |
| `tests/unit/*.py` | Unit suite (range, git, render, cli) | Authored |
| `tests/integration/test_git_real.py` | Real-git integration suite | Authored |
| `tests/e2e/test_cli_e2e.py` | CLI E2E suite | Authored |
| `tests/conftest.py` | Fixture-repo builder helper | Authored |
| `README.md` | Usage / dev docs | Authored |

**Status legend:** _Authored_ = file written and traced against acceptance
criteria by inspection. Promotion to _Done_ requires a green run of all three
test tiers in a shell-capable environment (this workspace has file tools only â€”
no `pytest`/`git` execution). See "Verification" below.

## Verification

All source and tests are written to this spec. Because this workspace exposes
file tools only (no shell), the suites have **not been executed here**. To
verify and promote to Done, run in a shell with `git` on PATH:

```bash
uv sync
uv run pytest                          # unit tier â€” must be green
uv run pytest -m integration           # real git against fixture repo
uv run pytest -m e2e                   # installed CLI end to end
```

Then commit per the plan's TDD ordering and proceed to `/orchestra-review`.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Functional
- [ ] `relnotes v1.2.0..v1.3.0` prints markdown listing every commit reachable from `v1.3.0` but not `v1.2.0`.
- [ ] `relnotes v1.2.0` defaults the upper bound to `HEAD`.
- [ ] Output is valid markdown: a title (range + generation date) and one bullet per commit (subject + short SHA), to stdout.
- [ ] Unknown tag â†’ exit non-zero, clear message on stderr, no traceback.
- [ ] Empty range â†’ exit non-zero with a clear message.
- [ ] Run outside a git repo â†’ clear error, non-zero exit.
- [ ] `git` not on PATH â†’ clear error, non-zero exit.
- [ ] `relnotes --help` and `relnotes --version` work.

### Unit
- [ ] Range parser accepts `A..B` and bare `A`; rejects `..B`, `A..`, `A...B`, empty.
- [ ] Git wrapper parses canned `git log` output into `Commit` objects correctly.
- [ ] Each error class maps to the documented exit code and stderr message (git mocked).
- [ ] Renderer output is deterministic with the clock monkeypatched.

### Integration
- [ ] Against a real fixture repo, `read_commits` returns the correct commits for `A..B` and bare `A`.
- [ ] Unknown tag and empty range are detected against the real `git` binary.

### E2E
- [ ] Installed `relnotes` console script produces correct stdout/exit code for success, unknown tag, and outside-repo cases.

## Dependencies

- `git` binary on PATH (runtime). `uv`, `pytest` (dev).
- No step depends on a later step's output (Steps 1â†’8 are dependency-ordered).

## Risks

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|
| `git log` output format varies / commit text contains delimiters | Use `%x1f`/`%x1e` control bytes that cannot appear in commit text; verify shape in the integration tier, not just mocks |
| Distinguishing "unknown ref" from "empty range" from raw git errors | Pre-verify refs with `rev-parse --verify`; treat zero commits on a valid range as the explicit empty-range case |
| Mocked subprocess tests drift from real git behavior | Mandatory integration tier hits the real `git` binary against a fixture repo |
| Console-script entry point misconfigured | E2E tier invokes the installed script as a subprocess, catching wiring errors |
| Over-broad range syntax creeping into M1 | Range parser is a closed whitelist (`A..B`, bare `A`); everything else is a usage error |

/.orchestra/README.md:
# .orchestra/

Agent knowledge base for this project. All agents read this folder before acting.

## Structure

- `roadmap.md` â€” The score. Project vision and milestone index.
- `adr/` â€” Architecture Decision Records. Decisions that affect future agents.
- `work/` â€” Per-work-item folders. Each contains a PRD, spec, and gherkin.
- `uml/` â€” Mermaid diagrams for architecture, workflows, and state machines.
- `devlog/` â€” Chronological journal of development sessions.

## The Loop

```
/orchestra-roadmap   â†’ define vision and milestones
/orchestra-plan      â†’ PRD â†’ Spec â†’ Gherkin for a work item
/orchestra-implement â†’ execute an approved spec
/orchestra-review    â†’ validate implementation against spec
/orchestra-merge     â†’ merge and close the work item
```

## Rules

- Agents read the roadmap and relevant ADRs before acting on any work item
- Every work item has a PRD before a spec; every spec before implementation
- Nothing moves forward without explicit human approval at each gate
/.orchestra/roadmap.md:
# relnotes Roadmap

**Objective:** A developer runs `relnotes v1.2.0..v1.3.0` and gets clean,
well-organized markdown release notes â€” changes grouped by type, noise commits
dropped, breaking changes highlighted â€” ready to paste into a changelog or
GitHub release.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Given any two reachable tags, the tool emits valid markdown release notes without manual editing
- [ ] Commits are grouped into meaningful categories (features, fixes, etc.)
- [ ] Noise commits (merges, chores, WIP) are dropped by default
- [ ] Breaking changes are detected and surfaced in a dedicated section
- [ ] The tool is installable and documented for end users

## Context

Maintainers and release managers today either hand-write release notes or paste
raw `git log` output. `relnotes` automates the curation: it reads the commit
history between two tags, classifies and groups the commits, removes noise, and
highlights anything that breaks compatibility â€” producing polished markdown in
one command.

## Milestones

| Material | Location | Status |
|----------|----------|--------|
| M1 â€” Walking skeleton: tag range â†’ commits â†’ flat markdown | .orchestra/work/m1-skeleton/prd.md | Code complete â€” awaiting test run |
| M2 â€” Curation: grouping, noise filtering, breaking-change highlighting | .orchestra/work/m2-curation/prd.md | Not Started |
| M3 â€” Configuration & output options | .orchestra/work/m3-config/prd.md | Not Started |
| M4 â€” Distribution & documentation | .orchestra/work/m4-distribution/prd.md | Not Started |

## References

- ADR-000: [The Score](.orchestra/adr/ADR-000-the-score.md)
/src/relnotes/__init__.py:
"""relnotes â€” polished markdown release notes from a git tag range."""

__version__ = "0.1.0"
/src/relnotes/__main__.py:
"""Enable ``python -m relnotes``."""

import sys

from .cli import main

if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())
/src/relnotes/cli.py:
"""Command-line entry point for relnotes.

This is the only module that knows about ``argv``, stdout/stderr, and exit
codes. It wires the pipeline together:

    parse args -> parse_range -> assert_repo -> verify_ref x2
                -> read_commits -> render -> print

Every :class:`RelnotesError` is caught here and rendered as a single clean
``relnotes: error: ...`` line on stderr, returning the error's exit code. No
traceback ever reaches the user for an expected failure.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import sys
from typing import List, Optional

from . import __version__, git
from .errors import EmptyRangeError, RelnotesError
from .ranges import parse_range
from .render import render


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="relnotes",
        description="Generate polished markdown release notes from a git tag range.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "range",
        metavar="RANGE",
        help="Tag range 'A..B', or a single ref 'A' (treated as 'A..HEAD').",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--version",
        action="version",
        version=f"relnotes {__version__}",
    )
    return parser


def main(argv: Optional[List[str]] = None) -> int:
    """Run relnotes. Returns the process exit code."""
    argv = sys.argv[1:] if argv is None else argv
    parser = build_parser()

    try:
        args = parser.parse_args(argv)
    except SystemExit as exc:
        # argparse handles --help/--version (0) and bad usage (2) itself.
        return int(exc.code) if exc.code is not None else 0

    try:
        low, high = parse_range(args.range)
        git.assert_repo()
        git.verify_ref(low)
        git.verify_ref(high)
        commits = git.read_commits(low, high)
        if not commits:
            raise EmptyRangeError(
                f"no commits found in range {low}..{high}"
            )
        sys.stdout.write(render(f"{low}..{high}", commits))
        sys.stdout.write("\n")
        return 0
    except RelnotesError as exc:
        print(f"relnotes: error: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
        return exc.exit_code


if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover
    sys.exit(main())
/src/relnotes/errors.py:
"""Error hierarchy for relnotes.

Each error carries the process exit code the CLI should return when it
propagates. ``UsageError`` mirrors argparse's convention of exit code 2 for
bad invocation; all other operational failures use exit code 1.
"""

from __future__ import annotations


class RelnotesError(Exception):
    """Base class for all expected, user-facing relnotes failures.

    These are caught at the CLI boundary and rendered as a clean
    ``relnotes: error: ...`` message rather than a traceback.
    """

    exit_code = 1


class UsageError(RelnotesError):
    """The command was invoked incorrectly (e.g. malformed range syntax)."""

    exit_code = 2


class NotARepoError(RelnotesError):
    """relnotes was run outside a git repository."""


class GitNotFoundError(RelnotesError):
    """The ``git`` binary could not be found on PATH."""


class UnknownRefError(RelnotesError):
    """A supplied ref (tag/branch/revision) does not exist in the repo."""


class EmptyRangeError(RelnotesError):
    """The range is valid but contains no commits."""
/src/relnotes/git.py:
"""The single boundary between relnotes and the ``git`` binary.

No other module shells out. Everything here goes through :func:`_run`, which
keeps the subprocess call in one place so unit tests can mock it and the
integration tier can exercise the real thing.

The commit log is requested in a delimiter-safe format: fields are joined with
the ASCII Unit Separator (``\\x1f``) and records terminated with the Record
Separator (``\\x1e``). Those control bytes cannot appear in commit text, so a
subject containing ``|``, ``..``, or other punctuation never breaks parsing.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import subprocess

from .errors import GitNotFoundError, NotARepoError, UnknownRefError
from .models import Commit

US = "\x1f"  # field separator (ASCII Unit Separator)
RS = "\x1e"  # record separator (ASCII Record Separator)

_FORMAT = US.join(["%H", "%h", "%s", "%an", "%aI"]) + RS


def _run(args: list[str]) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
    """Run ``git`` with the given arguments, capturing output as text.

    Raises:
        GitNotFoundError: if the ``git`` executable is not on PATH.
    """
    try:
        return subprocess.run(
            ["git", *args],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
        )
    except FileNotFoundError as exc:  # git not installed / not on PATH
        raise GitNotFoundError(
            "could not find 'git' on PATH; install git and try again"
        ) from exc


def assert_repo() -> None:
    """Verify the current directory is inside a git work tree.

    Raises:
        NotARepoError: if not inside a git repository.
    """
    result = _run(["rev-parse", "--git-dir"])
    if result.returncode != 0:
        raise NotARepoError(
            "not a git repository; run relnotes from inside a git repo"
        )


def verify_ref(ref: str) -> None:
    """Verify that ``ref`` resolves to a commit.

    Raises:
        UnknownRefError: if the ref does not exist.
    """
    result = _run(["rev-parse", "--verify", "--quiet", f"{ref}^{{commit}}"])
    if result.returncode != 0:
        raise UnknownRefError(f"unknown git ref: {ref!r}")


def read_commits(low: str, high: str) -> list[Commit]:
    """Return the commits in ``low..high``, newest first.

    Assumes both refs have already been verified with :func:`verify_ref`.

    Raises:
        UnknownRefError: if git rejects the range despite verification.
    """
    result = _run(["log", f"{low}..{high}", f"--pretty=format:{_FORMAT}"])
    if result.returncode != 0:
        raise UnknownRefError(
            f"git could not resolve range {low}..{high}: "
            f"{result.stderr.strip() or 'unknown error'}"
        )
    return _parse_log(result.stdout)


def _parse_log(output: str) -> list[Commit]:
    """Parse delimiter-framed ``git log`` output into :class:`Commit` objects."""
    commits: list[Commit] = []
    for record in output.split(RS):
        record = record.strip("\n")
        if not record:
            continue
        fields = record.split(US)
        if len(fields) != 5:
            continue
        sha, short_sha, subject, author, iso_date = fields
        commits.append(
            Commit(
                sha=sha,
                short_sha=short_sha,
                subject=subject,
                author=author,
                iso_date=iso_date,
            )
        )
    return commits
/src/relnotes/models.py:
"""Domain models for relnotes."""

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Commit:
    """A single commit read from the git history.

    Attributes:
        sha: Full 40-character commit hash.
        short_sha: Abbreviated hash as git renders it (``%h``).
        subject: First line of the commit message (``%s``).
        author: Author name (``%an``).
        iso_date: Author date in strict ISO-8601 (``%aI``).
    """

    sha: str
    short_sha: str
    subject: str
    author: str
    iso_date: str
/src/relnotes/ranges.py:
"""Parsing of the relnotes range argument.

M1 supports exactly two forms:

* ``A..B`` â€” commits reachable from ``B`` but not ``A``.
* ``A``    â€” a single ref, treated as ``A..HEAD``.

Anything else (``...``, multiple ``..``, an empty side, or an empty string)
is a usage error. The surface is deliberately narrow; richer revision
selection is deferred to a later milestone.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from .errors import UsageError

_SUPPORTED = "supported forms are 'A..B' and 'A' (treated as 'A..HEAD')"


def parse_range(arg: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
    """Resolve a range argument into a ``(low, high)`` pair of refs.

    Raises:
        UsageError: if ``arg`` is empty or not one of the supported forms.
    """
    if arg is None:
        raise UsageError(f"no range given; {_SUPPORTED}")

    text = arg.strip()
    if not text:
        raise UsageError(f"empty range; {_SUPPORTED}")

    if "..." in text:
        raise UsageError(f"unsupported range {arg!r}; {_SUPPORTED}")

    if ".." in text:
        parts = text.split("..")
        if len(parts) != 2 or not parts[0] or not parts[1]:
            raise UsageError(f"malformed range {arg!r}; {_SUPPORTED}")
        return parts[0], parts[1]

    # Bare ref â†’ A..HEAD
    return text, "HEAD"
/src/relnotes/render.py:
"""Render commits into a flat markdown document.

M1 produces a deliberately simple shape: a title with the range, a generated-on
date, and one bullet per commit. Grouping, noise filtering, and breaking-change
highlighting arrive in M2 and will replace the body of this renderer.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Optional, Sequence

from .models import Commit


def render(
    range_str: str,
    commits: Sequence[Commit],
    now: Optional[datetime] = None,
) -> str:
    """Return markdown release notes for ``commits`` over ``range_str``.

    Args:
        range_str: Human-readable range, e.g. ``"v1.2.0..v1.3.0"``.
        commits: Commits to list, in the order they should appear.
        now: Generation timestamp; defaults to the current UTC time. Injected
            for deterministic tests.
    """
    if now is None:
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    lines = [
        f"# Release notes for {range_str}",
        "",
        f"_Generated on {now.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')}_",
        "",
    ]
    for commit in commits:
        lines.append(f"- {commit.subject} ({commit.short_sha})")
    lines.append("")
    return "\n".join(lines)
/tests/e2e/test_cli_e2e.py:
"""End-to-end tests invoking the installed relnotes console script.

Requires the package to be installed (e.g. ``uv sync`` / ``pip install -e .``)
so the ``relnotes`` console script is on PATH. Skipped cleanly otherwise.
"""

import shutil
import subprocess

import pytest

RELNOTES = shutil.which("relnotes")

pytestmark = [
    pytest.mark.e2e,
    pytest.mark.skipif(
        RELNOTES is None,
        reason="relnotes console script not installed; run `uv sync` first",
    ),
]


def _run(cwd, *args):
    return subprocess.run(
        [RELNOTES, *args],
        cwd=cwd,
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
    )


def test_success_prints_markdown(fixture_repo):
    fixture_repo.commit("Initial", tag="v1")
    fixture_repo.commit("Add feature", tag="v2")
    result = _run(fixture_repo.path, "v1..v2")
    assert result.returncode == 0
    assert "# Release notes for v1..v2" in result.stdout
    assert "- Add feature" in result.stdout


def test_bare_ref_defaults_to_head(fixture_repo):
    fixture_repo.commit("Initial", tag="v1")
    fixture_repo.commit("Later change")
    result = _run(fixture_repo.path, "v1")
    assert result.returncode == 0
    assert "- Later change" in result.stdout


def test_unknown_tag_errors(fixture_repo):
    fixture_repo.commit("Initial", tag="v1")
    result = _run(fixture_repo.path, "v1..v999")
    assert result.returncode != 0
    assert "unknown" in result.stderr.lower()
    assert "Traceback" not in result.stderr


def test_outside_repo_errors(tmp_path):
    result = _run(tmp_path, "v1..v2")
    assert result.returncode != 0
    assert "git repository" in result.stderr.lower()


def test_version(tmp_path):
    result = _run(tmp_path, "--version")
    assert result.returncode == 0
    assert "relnotes" in result.stdout
/tests/integration/test_git_real.py:
"""Integration tests: the real git binary against a real fixture repo.

No mocks at the git boundary â€” this tier verifies the assumptions the unit
tests encode (log output shape, ref resolution, exit codes).
"""

import pytest

from relnotes import git
from relnotes.errors import UnknownRefError

pytestmark = pytest.mark.integration


def test_explicit_range_newest_first(fixture_repo, monkeypatch):
    fixture_repo.commit("Initial", tag="v1")
    fixture_repo.commit("Add feature")
    fixture_repo.commit("Fix bug", tag="v2")
    monkeypatch.chdir(fixture_repo.path)

    git.assert_repo()
    git.verify_ref("v1")
    git.verify_ref("v2")
    commits = git.read_commits("v1", "v2")

    assert [c.subject for c in commits] == ["Fix bug", "Add feature"]
    # Real short SHAs are non-empty and a prefix of the full SHA.
    for c in commits:
        assert c.short_sha and c.sha.startswith(c.short_sha)


def test_bare_ref_resolves_to_head(fixture_repo, monkeypatch):
    fixture_repo.commit("Initial", tag="v1")
    fixture_repo.commit("After one")
    fixture_repo.commit("After two")
    monkeypatch.chdir(fixture_repo.path)

    commits = git.read_commits("v1", "HEAD")
    assert [c.subject for c in commits] == ["After two", "After one"]


def test_unknown_ref_raises(fixture_repo, monkeypatch):
    fixture_repo.commit("Initial", tag="v1")
    monkeypatch.chdir(fixture_repo.path)
    with pytest.raises(UnknownRefError):
        git.verify_ref("v999")


def test_empty_range_returns_no_commits(fixture_repo, monkeypatch):
    fixture_repo.commit("Initial", tag="v1")
    monkeypatch.chdir(fixture_repo.path)
    assert git.read_commits("v1", "v1") == []
/tests/unit/test_cli.py:
"""Unit tests for the CLI wiring with the git layer mocked."""

from relnotes import cli, git
from relnotes.errors import UnknownRefError
from relnotes.models import Commit


def _stub_git(monkeypatch, commits):
    monkeypatch.setattr(git, "assert_repo", lambda: None)
    monkeypatch.setattr(git, "verify_ref", lambda ref: None)
    monkeypatch.setattr(git, "read_commits", lambda low, high: commits)


def test_success_prints_markdown(monkeypatch, capsys):
    _stub_git(monkeypatch, [Commit("f", "abc123", "Add x", "A", "2024-01-01T00:00:00+00:00")])
    code = cli.main(["v1..v2"])
    out = capsys.readouterr().out
    assert code == 0
    assert "# Release notes for v1..v2" in out
    assert "- Add x (abc123)" in out


def test_empty_range_errors(monkeypatch, capsys):
    _stub_git(monkeypatch, [])
    code = cli.main(["v1..v2"])
    err = capsys.readouterr().err
    assert code == 1
    assert "no commits" in err.lower()


def test_unknown_ref_errors(monkeypatch, capsys):
    monkeypatch.setattr(git, "assert_repo", lambda: None)

    def bad(ref):
        raise UnknownRefError(f"unknown git ref: {ref!r}")

    monkeypatch.setattr(git, "verify_ref", bad)
    code = cli.main(["v9.9.9..v2"])
    err = capsys.readouterr().err
    assert code == 1
    assert "unknown" in err.lower()
    assert "Traceback" not in err


def test_malformed_range_exits_2(capsys):
    code = cli.main(["v1...v2"])
    err = capsys.readouterr().err
    assert code == 2
    assert "relnotes: error:" in err


def test_version_flag(capsys):
    code = cli.main(["--version"])
    out = capsys.readouterr().out
    assert code == 0
    assert "relnotes" in out


def test_help_flag(capsys):
    code = cli.main(["--help"])
    out = capsys.readouterr().out
    assert code == 0
    assert "RANGE" in out
/tests/unit/test_render.py:
"""Unit tests for the markdown renderer (clock injected for determinism)."""

from datetime import datetime, timezone

from relnotes.models import Commit
from relnotes.render import render

FIXED_NOW = datetime(2024, 6, 13, 12, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)


def _commit(subject, short_sha):
    return Commit(
        sha="full" + short_sha,
        short_sha=short_sha,
        subject=subject,
        author="Author",
        iso_date="2024-01-01T00:00:00+00:00",
    )


def test_title_and_date():
    out = render("v1..v2", [_commit("Add x", "abc123")], now=FIXED_NOW)
    assert "# Release notes for v1..v2" in out
    assert "_Generated on 2024-06-13_" in out


def test_one_bullet_per_commit_in_order():
    commits = [_commit("Add feature", "abc123"), _commit("Fix bug", "def456")]
    out = render("v1..v2", commits, now=FIXED_NOW)
    assert "- Add feature (abc123)" in out
    assert "- Fix bug (def456)" in out
    assert out.index("Add feature") < out.index("Fix bug")


def test_output_is_string_ending_in_newline():
    out = render("v1..v2", [_commit("Add x", "abc123")], now=FIXED_NOW)
    assert out.endswith("\n")
/tests/conftest.py:
"""Shared test fixtures.

``fixture_repo`` builds a real, throwaway git repository in ``tmp_path`` for the
integration and e2e tiers â€” no mocks at the git boundary.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import subprocess
from pathlib import Path

import pytest


class FixtureRepo:
    """A small real git repo with a helper to add tagged commits."""

    def __init__(self, path: Path) -> None:
        self.path = path

    def _git(self, *args: str) -> None:
        subprocess.run(
            ["git", *args],
            cwd=self.path,
            check=True,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
        )

    def commit(self, message: str, tag: str | None = None) -> None:
        (self.path / "file.txt").write_text(message + "\n")
        self._git("add", ".")
        self._git("commit", "-q", "-m", message)
        if tag:
            self._git("tag", tag)


@pytest.fixture
def fixture_repo(tmp_path: Path) -> FixtureRepo:
    repo_path = tmp_path / "repo"
    repo_path.mkdir()
    repo = FixtureRepo(repo_path)
    repo._git("init", "-q")
    repo._git("config", "user.email", "test@example.com")
    repo._git("config", "user.name", "Test User")
    repo._git("config", "commit.gpgsign", "false")
    return repo

/CLAUDE.md:
# CLAUDE.md

## Orchestra SDLC â€” required workflow

This project follows the Orchestra SDLC, served by the `orchestra-sdlc` MCP server.
The `.orchestra/` directory is the agent knowledge base â€” read `roadmap.md` and
relevant ADRs before acting on any work item.

Before starting any non-trivial work:

1. Call `orchestra_list_skills` to see the available playbooks.
2. Call `orchestra_get_skill` for the relevant activity (PRD, spec, gherkin,
   implement, review, merge, â€¦) and follow it as the playbook.
3. Respect the gates (`orchestra_get_gates`): every work item has a PRD before
   a spec, a spec before implementation. Nothing advances without explicit
   human approval at each gate.

Record sessions with `orchestra_devlog_entry` (write the returned file), and
record significant decisions as ADRs in `.orchestra/adr/`.
/README.md:
# relnotes

> A command-line tool that turns a git tag range into polished, human-readable release notes in markdown.

## Brief

**Vision:** A developer runs `relnotes v1.2.0..v1.3.0` and gets clean,
well-organized markdown release notes â€” changes grouped by type, noise commits
dropped, breaking changes highlighted â€” ready to paste into a changelog or
GitHub release. No manual curation required.

**Audience:** Maintainers and release managers of git-based software projects
who today hand-write release notes or paste raw `git log` output.

## Usage

> **Requires `git` on your PATH** at runtime â€” `relnotes` shells out to it.

```bash
# Notes for everything between two tags
relnotes v1.2.0..v1.3.0

# Single ref is treated as <ref>..HEAD
relnotes v1.2.0

# Redirect into a changelog
relnotes v1.2.0..v1.3.0 > NOTES.md
```

Example output (M1 â€” flat list; grouping & breaking-change highlighting land in M2):

```markdown
# Release notes for v1.2.0..v1.3.0

_Generated on 2025-06-13_

- Add JSON output flag (a1b2c3d)
- Fix crash on empty range (e4f5a6b)
```

Exit codes: `0` success Â· `2` bad invocation (malformed range / missing arg) Â·
`1` operational error (unknown tag, empty range, not a git repo, `git` missing).

## Development

This is a Python package built with [`uv`](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) and tested with `pytest`.

```bash
uv sync                              # install package + dev deps (editable)

uv run pytest                        # unit tier (default; fast, fully mocked)
uv run pytest -m integration         # integration tier (real git, fixture repo)
uv run pytest -m e2e                 # e2e tier (installed CLI, real stack)
uv run pytest -m "integration or e2e"  # all live tiers
```

See [`.orchestra/`](.orchestra/) for the roadmap, decisions (ADRs), and per-milestone specs.
/pyproject.toml:
[project]
name = "relnotes"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Generate polished markdown release notes from a git tag range."
readme = "README.md"
requires-python = ">=3.9"
license = { text = "MIT" }
dependencies = []

[project.scripts]
relnotes = "relnotes.cli:main"

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = ["pytest>=7"]

[build-system]
requires = ["hatchling"]
build-backend = "hatchling.build"

[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
packages = ["src/relnotes"]

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["tests"]
# Default run excludes the live tiers; opt in with `-m integration` / `-m e2e`.
addopts = "-m 'not integration and not e2e'"
markers = [
    "integration: hits the real git binary against a fixture repo (excluded by default)",
    "e2e: invokes the installed relnotes console script (excluded by default)",
]
